title: hieracium villosum subsp. undulifolium
intro: Hieracium villosum subsp. undulifolium, also known as the hairy hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion-like hawkweed family (Asteraceae). The hairy hawkweed has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits include potential use as a wound healer, reducing inflammation, and treating skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make a medicinal tea, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
